skill.name: picture-book-wizard
skill.description: Creates bilingual Chinese and English children's picture book drafts with age-aware story text, pinyin, learning points, and image prompts for consistent characters.
skill.summary: Generate bilingual children's picture book stories, learning notes, and consistent image prompts.
skill.author: kart-io

security_audit.summary: AI review found that most static alerts are false positives from Markdown code fences, documentation examples, YAML prose, and words such as same. The repository does include local shell utility scripts, including one that can delete fixed documentation files, so publication is acceptable with a medium-risk warning. No evidence found of malicious intent, credential exfiltration, prompt injection, or runtime network callbacks.

medium_findings.title: Local cleanup script deletes fixed documentation files
medium_findings.description: The cleanup utility contains a fixed list of documentation files and removes them when run without --dry-run. The targets are repository-local guide files, so this is not evidence of malicious deletion, but it is a real filesystem side effect that users should review before running.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly invokes rm on files from a fixed list under the skill directory. The limited target list reduces severity, but the destructive operation is real.

low_findings.title: Static command-execution alerts are mostly Markdown examples
low_findings.description: Many external command findings point to fenced Markdown examples or output templates rather than executable code. These examples show usage commands or prompt output formats and are not automatically executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown code fences and documentation examples, not runtime code paths. This matches the repeated analyzer pattern across documentation files.

low_findings.title: Sensitive-data alerts are false positives from prose
low_findings.description: The critical Windows SAM detections appear to be substring matches in ordinary words and prose, such as repeated use of same in character-consistency documentation. No evidence found of Windows registry, SAM database access, credential file reads, or secret extraction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found ordinary content about keeping visual features the same. No credential-access API or sensitive path was present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Network indicators are documentation URLs, not callbacks
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL evidence is an installation command that clones the public source repository. No evidence found of runtime network requests, telemetry endpoints, or data exfiltration behavior in the reviewed Python engine files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is inside installation documentation. The reviewed engine imports local configuration and assembles prompts without network client use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell script with repository-local deletion
dangerous_patterns.description: A utility script uses shell logic and rm to remove a predefined set of duplicate documentation files. It should not be run automatically during normal skill use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell script and rm operation are directly visible. The risk is bounded because paths are fixed relative to the script directory.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell pipelines over repository files
dangerous_patterns.description: A documentation statistics utility uses find, xargs, wc, sort, head, and tail over repository Markdown files. This is normal maintenance tooling, but it is still executable shell code in the package.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and operate on local Markdown files. No user-controlled command string or network operation was found in this script.

content.value_statement: Creating consistent bilingual picture books for children is slow when story text, pinyin, learning goals, and image prompts must align. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to produce age-aware story pages with repeatable visual direction.
actual_capabilities: Generates Chinese and English story text for children ages 3 to 12.
Adds pinyin, one Chinese learning point, and optional learning objectives per page.
Builds image prompts for 18 supported visual styles and 12 scene types.
Uses character consistency guidance for recurring children, relatives, and animal companions.
Supports cultural styles such as ink, nianhua, porcelain, and shadow puppet.
Provides examples and reference guides for extending styles, scenes, and characters.
limitations: It does not generate final images by itself; it prepares prompts for an image model.
Generated educational content still needs adult review before classroom or publishing use.
The included scripts are maintenance utilities and should be reviewed before running.
Character and style consistency depends on the downstream image generator following prompts.

use_cases.title: Draft a Bilingual Bedtime Story
use_cases.description: Create short Chinese and English story pages with pinyin and gentle image prompts for a young child.

use_cases.title: Prepare Classroom Reading Material
use_cases.description: Generate age-appropriate pages that combine story text, vocabulary focus, and simple learning goals.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Picture Book Concept
use_cases.description: Explore styles, scenes, characters, and page structures before commissioning art or producing final layouts.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Story Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Start with a simple three-page book.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use picture-book-wizard to create a watercolor meadow story for age 4 with the default character. Include Chinese, English, pinyin, one learning point, and image prompts.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Cultural Style
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a culturally themed book concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a nianhua kitchen picture book for age 6 with Yueyue and grandma. Keep the tone warm and include one cultural object learning point per page.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Character Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan a recurring cast across several pages.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a five-page clay forest story for age 8 with Xiaoming, a dog companion, and dad. Maintain clear character anchors across every image prompt.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Educational Theme
prompt_templates.scenario: Combine story, learning goals, and visual direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a seven-page ink pond story for age 10 about reflection and observation. Add science learning notes, bilingual text, pinyin, and strict consistency prompts.

output_examples.output: A short bilingual page sequence with simple Chinese sentences, English translations, pinyin, one character focus, and soft meadow image prompts.

output_examples.input: nianhua kitchen story with grandma for age 6
output_examples.output: A festive family story with cultural objects, Chinese and English text, pinyin support, and consistent visual prompts for Yueyue and grandma.

output_examples.input: clay forest story with a recurring animal companion
output_examples.output: A multi-page adventure outline that keeps the child and companion visually consistent while changing actions and expressions.

best_practices: Specify style, scene, age, page count, and character before asking for a full book.
Review all bilingual text, pinyin, and learning notes before sharing with children.
Use the first page as the visual anchor when sending prompts to an image generator.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for final images if the active tool cannot generate images.
Do not mix many styles in one book unless you want experimental visual results.
Do not run maintenance scripts without reading their file operations first.

faq.question: Does this skill create finished illustrated books?
faq.answer: It creates story text, learning notes, and image prompts. A separate image model or illustrator creates the final images.

faq.question: Which languages does it support?
faq.answer: It focuses on Chinese and English bilingual picture books with pinyin for Chinese pronunciation support.

faq.question: Can I choose the art style?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents 18 styles, including watercolor, clay, paper-cut, ink, nianhua, porcelain, and tech.

faq.question: Can it make books for older children?
faq.answer: Yes. The age system covers ages 3 to 12 and adjusts pages, vocabulary, and learning goals.

faq.question: Are the included scripts required for normal use?
faq.answer: No. They are maintenance scripts for repository cleanup and documentation statistics, not core story generation.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish in the marketplace?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because the repository includes local shell scripts with filesystem operations.
